creator: Ivan Mehta
title: (No AI) Google launches Lyria 3 Pro music generation model
dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
content: Google is launching Lyria 3 Pro, an upgraded music model that generates longer, more customizable tracks, as it expands AI music tools across Gemini, enterprise products, and other services.
contentSnippet: Google is launching Lyria 3 Pro, an upgraded music model that generates longer, more customizable tracks, as it expands AI music tools across Gemini, enterprise products, and other services.

creator: Sarah Perez
title: (No AI) Reddit takes on the bots with new ‘human verification’ requirements for fishy behavior
dc:creator: Sarah Perez
content: Reddit will require suspected automated accounts to verify they’re human, as it ramps up efforts to curb bot-driven spam and manipulation.
contentSnippet: Reddit will require suspected automated accounts to verify they’re human, as it ramps up efforts to curb bot-driven spam and manipulation.

title: (No AI) Reddit accounts with ‘fishy’ bot-like behavior will soon need to prove they’re human
author: Emma Roth
content:
![An image showing the Reddit logo on a red and white background](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25137774/STK115_Reddit_04.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Reddit is taking new steps to identify bots on the platform - a process that may require some users to confirm that they're human. In [a post on Wednesday](https://www.reddit.com/user/spez/comments/1s3ezrc/humans_welcome_bots_must_wear_name_tags/), Reddit CEO Steve Huffman writes that the company will introduce a labeling system for accounts registered as bots, and ask users with "automated" or "fishy behavior" to verify that they're human using methods like fingerprint scanning or submitting their ID.

With this update, developers can register automated accounts with Reddit, which will then receive an "\[APP\]" label. However, Reddit also notes that it will be on the lookout for unlabeled accounts with suspicious behavior. "If somethi …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/900363/reddit-human-verification-bots-crackdown)
contentSnippet:
Reddit is taking new steps to identify bots on the platform - a process that may require some users to confirm that they're human. In a post on Wednesday, Reddit CEO Steve Huffman writes that the company will introduce a labeling system for accounts registered as bots, and ask users with "automated" or "fishy behavior" to verify that they're human using methods like fingerprint scanning or submitting their ID.
With this update, developers can register automated accounts with Reddit, which will then receive an "[APP]" label. However, Reddit also notes that it will be on the lookout for unlabeled accounts with suspicious behavior. "If somethi …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Google Lyria 3 Pro makes longer AI songs
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![An illustration of the Google logo on a red, green, and blue background](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/03/STK093_GOOGLE_A-1.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Six times the slop. | Image: The Verge

Google is expanding the capabilities of its Lyria 3 music-making AI, enabling it to create tracks up to three minutes long and from within multiple other Google Products. Until now, Lyria had been limited to 30-second clips. Lyria 3 Pro not only increases the maximum length sixfold, it also allows the user to prompt for specific elements like intros, choruses, and bridges for greater control over arrangements.

Lyria 3 Pro works much like other popular music generation tools, such as Suno and Udio. Describe a mood, style, or instrumentation, and it spits out a track. It can also generate lyrics based on your prompt, or even a reference photo …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/900425/google-lyria-3-pro-ai-music)

title: (No AI) Inside our approach to the Model Spec
content: Learn how OpenAI’s Model Spec serves as a public framework for model behavior, balancing safety, user freedom, and accountability as AI systems advance.
contentSnippet: Learn how OpenAI’s Model Spec serves as a public framework for model behavior, balancing safety, user freedom, and accountability as AI systems advance.

title: (No AI) Introducing the OpenAI Safety Bug Bounty program
content: OpenAI launches a Safety Bug Bounty program to identify AI abuse and safety risks, including agentic vulnerabilities, prompt injection, and data exfiltration.
contentSnippet: OpenAI launches a Safety Bug Bounty program to identify AI abuse and safety risks, including agentic vulnerabilities, prompt injection, and data exfiltration.

title: (No AI) Lyria 3 Pro: Create longer tracks in more
content: Introducing Lyria 3 Pro, which unlocks longer tracks with structural awareness. We’re also bringing Lyria to more Google products and surfaces.
contentSnippet: Introducing Lyria 3 Pro, which unlocks longer tracks with structural awareness. We’re also bringing Lyria to more Google products and surfaces.

title: (No AI) Measuring progress toward AGI: A cognitive framework
content: We’re introducing a framework to measure progress toward AGI, and launching a Kaggle hackathon to build the relevant evaluations.
contentSnippet: We’re introducing a framework to measure progress toward AGI, and launching a Kaggle hackathon to build the relevant evaluations.

creator: Maxwell Zeff
title: (No AI) OpenAI Enters Its Focus Era by Killing Sora
dc:creator: Maxwell Zeff
content: As the ChatGPT-maker eyes an IPO, it's ditching Sora in favor of a unified AI assistant and enterprise coding tools.
contentSnippet: As the ChatGPT-maker eyes an IPO, it's ditching Sora in favor of a unified AI assistant and enterprise coding tools.

creator: Molly Taft
title: (No AI) New Bernie Sanders AI Safety Bill Would Halt Data Center Construction
dc:creator: Molly Taft
content: The US senator said on Tuesday that a moratorium would give lawmakers time to "ensure that AI is safe." Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez will introduce a similar bill in the House in the weeks ahead.
contentSnippet: The US senator said on Tuesday that a moratorium would give lawmakers time to "ensure that AI is safe." Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez will introduce a similar bill in the House in the weeks ahead.
